ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for branded fast-food fried chicken bucket; SNOMED CT coverage for commercial fast-food products is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is an 8-piece bucket of spicy fried chicken, weighing approximately 1200 g total, typically shared among 3–4 people as a main meal.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat and skin, cooked, fried, batter (NDB No. 05062)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central batter-fried chicken (NDB 05062 / FDC ID 171477) as the closest approved-source equivalent to Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken. Input per_100g values (energy 233 kcal, protein 15.3 g, fat 15.3 g, carbohydrate 8.7 g, sodium 533 mg) are broadly consistent with USDA batter-fried chicken data and have been retained with minor rounding. Sodium is notably elevated relative to USDA generic values, consistent with Jollibee's seasoning blend; value retained from input with USDA as anchor. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and several micronutrients set to -1 where not reported in the input and not directly mappable from the USDA entry without further breakdown.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on the analogous food 'fried chicken with breaded coating' from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed GI tables (Atkinson et al. 2008). The breaded wheat-flour coating contributes the primary glycaemic load; the high protein and fat content of the chicken meat suppresses the overall GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (45) × carbohydrate per serving (104.4 g from 1200 g × 8.7 g/100 g) / 100 = 47.0 GL units.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (533 mg/100 g) from seasoning blend and salt, combined with saturated fat (4.7 g/100 g) contributing to arterial stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to 1–2 pieces maximum per occasion, remove the skin and breading to reduce sodium and saturated fat intake, and avoid pairing with high-sodium dipping sauces, as a single piece can contribute 200–300 mg sodium toward the 1500–2000 mg daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate breaded coating with very low dietary fibre (0.3 g/100 g) causing glycaemic instability, compounded by trans fat (0.2 g/100 g) worsening insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this food due to the refined wheat-flour coating raising postprandial blood glucose, the negligible fibre content (0.3 g/100 g) providing no glycaemic buffering, and the trans fat content further impairing insulin sensitivity; grilled skinless chicken is a strongly preferred alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of saturated fat (4.7 g/100 g), trans fat (0.2 g/100 g), high sodium (533 mg/100 g), and cholesterol (86.7 mg/100 g) from deep-frying and seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of this food, as the saturated fat and trans fat raise LDL cholesterol, the high sodium elevates blood pressure, and the cholesterol content (86.7 mg/100 g) adds cumulative cardiovascular risk; if consumed, limit to one piece and remove the skin and breading.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that worsen joint inflammation; trans fat (0.2 g/100 g) is strongly pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried foods entirely; the AGEs generated during deep-frying and the trans fat content directly exacerbate joint inflammation, and nightshade-derived spices (chilli powder, paprika) may additionally trigger sensitivity reactions in susceptible individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jollibee Chickenjoy Spicy Fried Chicken (8-Piece Bucket) is a signature menu item from Jollibee, the Filipino fast-food chain with outlets across Singapore. The product consists of eight bone-in chicken pieces (a mix of drumsticks, thighs, wings, and breast portions) marinated in a proprietary spicy seasoning blend featuring chilli powder, paprika, garlic, and black pepper, then coated in a seasoned wheat-flour and cornstarch batter and deep-fried in vegetable oil until the skin is exceptionally crispy and golden. The interior meat remains juicy and tender. The flavour profile is savoury, moderately spicy, and richly umami, with a pronounced crunch from the thick breaded coating. Per 100 g, the product provides approximately 233 kcal, 15.3 g protein, 15.3 g fat (4.7 g saturated, 0.2 g trans), and 8.7 g carbohydrate with minimal fibre (0.3 g). Sodium is very high at 533 mg/100 g. The 8-piece bucket weighs approximately 1200 g and is typically shared among 3–4 people. Clinically, this food is high concern for hypertension (very high sodium), type 2 diabetes (refined coating, negligible fibre, trans fat), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol). It is certified halal and contains gluten from the wheat-flour coating. It is a popular sharing meal for families and groups in Singapore's fast-food dining culture.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central batter-fried chicken (NDB 05062) as the closest approved-source equivalent. The input values from GrabFood (an unapproved delivery platform) are broadly consistent with USDA data and have been retained where plausible, but no direct approved-source measurement of Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken per 100 g is available. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown, mineral profile, and moisture data for Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024 at the branded product level. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed negligible/trace per USDA batter-fried chicken data; deep-fried chicken with breaded coating contains no meaningful vitamin C. Country of origin corrected from 'Singapore' (input) to 'Philippines (served in Singapore)' as Jollibee is a Filipino brand. Primary cuisine corrected from 'Burgers' (input error) to 'Filipino Fast Food'. Portion weight of 1200 g for 8 pieces retained from GrabFood input as plausible (approximately 150 g per piece). GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous breaded fried chicken from University of Sydney GI Database; glycemic load per serving = 47.0 GL units based on 104.4 g carbohydrate in 1200 g serving.
